parts.title: Part 1
parts.content:
Questions 1-6

Complete the form below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Guitar Group Coordinator: Gary [1] Level: [2] Place: the [3] [4] Street First floor, Room T347 Time: Thursday morning at [5] Recommended website: ‘The perfect [6] ’

Questions 7-10

Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. A typical 45-minute guitar lesson Time Activity Notes 5 minutes tuning guitars using an app or by [7] 10 minutes strumming chords using our thumbs keeping time while the teacher is [8] 15 minutes playing songs often listening to a [9] of a song 10 minutes playing single notes and simple tunes playing together, then [10] 5 minutes noting things to practise at home

parts.title: Part 2
parts.content:
Questions 11-16

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Working as a lifeboat volunteer

Questions 17-18

Choose TWO letters, A–E.

Questions 19-20

Choose TWO letters, A–E.

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 11-16

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Working as a lifeboat volunteer

contentBlocks.prompt: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?
options: A. He was eager to develop a hobby.
B. He wanted to work shorter hours.
C. He found his job in website design unsatisfying

contentBlocks.prompt: The Lifeboat Institution in Northsea was built with money provided by
options: A. a local organisation.
B. a local resident.
C. the local council.

contentBlocks.prompt: In his health assessment, the doctor was concerned about the fact that David
options: A. might be colour blind.
B. was rather short-sighted.
C. had undergone eye surgery.

contentBlocks.prompt: After arriving at the lifeboat station, they aim to launch the boat within
options: A. five minutes.
B. six to eight minutes.
C. eight and a half minutes.

contentBlocks.prompt: As a ‘helmsman’, David has the responsibility of deciding
options: A. who will be the members of his crew.
B. what equipment it will be necessary to take.
C. if the lifeboat should be launched.

contentBlocks.prompt: As well as going out on the lifeboat, David
options: A. gives talks on safety at sea.
B. helps with fundraising.
C. recruits new volunteers.

contentBlocks.prompt: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?
options: A. The residential course developed his leadership skills.
B. The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.
C. The training exercises have built up his mental strength.
D. The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.
E. The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.

contentBlocks.prompt: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?
options: A. working as part of a team
B. experiences when working in winter
C. being thanked by those he has helped
D. the fact that it keeps him fit
E. the chance to develop new equipment

parts.title: Part 3
parts.content:
Questions 21-24

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Questions 25-28

What reasons did the recycling manager give for rejecting footwear, according tothe students? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F. Reason Footwear

Questions 29-30

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 21-24

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

contentBlocks.prompt: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too
options: A. limited in scope.
B. hard to research.
C. boring for listeners.

contentBlocks.prompt: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about
options: A. how popular they are among young people.
B. how suitable they are for school.
C. how quickly they wear out.

contentBlocks.prompt: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because
options: A. they no longer fit.
B. she no longer likes them.
C. they are no longer in fashion.

contentBlocks.prompt: What did the article say that confused Don?
options: A. Public consumption of footwear has risen.
B. Less footwear is recycled now than in the past.
C. People dispose of more footwear than they used to.

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 25-28

What reasons did the recycling manager give for rejecting footwear, according tothe students?

Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F.

Reason

Footwear

options: A. one shoe was missing
B. the colour of one shoe had faded
C. one shoe had a hole in it
D. the shoes were brand new
E. he shoes were too dirty
F. the stitching on the shoes was broken

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 29-30

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

contentBlocks.prompt: Why did the project to make ‘new’ shoes out of old shoes fail?
options: A. People believed the 'new' pairs of shoes were unhygienic.
B. There were not enough good parts to use in the old shoes.
C. The shoes in the ‘new’ pairs were not completely alike.

contentBlocks.prompt: Bella and Don agree that they can present their topic
options: A. from a new angle.
B. with relevant images.
C. in a straightforward way.

parts.title: Part 4
parts.content:
Questions 31-40

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Tardigrades - more than 1,000 species, 0.05–1.2 millimetres long - also known as water ‘bears’ (due to how they [31] ) and ‘moss piglets’ Physical appearance - a [32] round body and four pairs of legs - claws or [33] for gripping - absence of respiratory organs - body filled with a liquid that carries both [34] and blood - mouth shaped like a [35] with teeth called stylets Habitat - often found at the bottom of a lake or on plants - very resilient and can exist in very low or high [36] Cryptobiosis - In dry conditions, they roll into a ball called a ‘tun’. - They stay alive with a much lower metabolism than usual. - A type of [37] ensures their DNA is not damaged. - Research is underway to find out how many days they can stay alive in [38] Feeding - consume liquids, e.g., those found in moss or [39] - may eat other tardigrades Conservation status - They are not considered to be [40].
